title: lepidium subcordatum
intro: Lepidium subcordatum, also known as Sea Currant or Sea Bladder, is a type of flowering plant in the mustard family (Brassicaceae) native to Australia. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly as a natural diuretic and to treat urinary tract issues, due to its high content of potassium and other minerals. The health benefits of Lepidium subcordatum include its ability to help with fluid retention, reduce blood pressure, and alleviate symptoms of cystitis and kidney stones. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support overall kidney and urinary health.
